column1: HUBMAP DATASET INTEGRATION PLAN FOR FUMA
CELLTYPE module uses a tree-multiselect widget. Each dataset is an <option> with a data-section attribute that defines its path in the tree.
Pattern for existing human datasets:  Tissue/Human  (e.g. "Kidney/Human")
Level1 = broad cell types (e.g. epithelial, immune, stromal)
Level2 = fine-grained subtypes (e.g. proximal tubule S1, S2, S3)
Each blade file = one tissue section: resources/views/celltype/celltype_options/{tissue}_options.blade.php
SNP2GENE module (chromatin/ATAC data) uses a flat grouped select in bed_annot.blade.php and epi_options.blade.php.
epi_options.blade.php    = Roadmap Epigenomics 15-core chromatin states (E001-E129, bulk tissue, not cell-type specific)
bed_annot.blade.php      = Additional BED annotations grouped by source: PsychENCODE, FANTOM5, Brain Open Chromatin Atlas (BOCA)
HuBMAP ATAC data would form a NEW group "HuBMAP Open Chromatin" within bed_annot.blade.php
Each entry = one gzipped BED file of open chromatin peaks for one cell type in one tissue
ACCESS NOTE: All HuBMAP datasets listed below are "protected" (controlled access).
Data access must be requested via https://portal.hubmapconsortium.org before download.
QUALITY FILTER APPLIED: Q30 >= 80% where available; all datasets have Published status.

column5: Existing in FUMA (CELLTYPE)

column1: SECTION 2: SNP2GENE MODULE (ATAC-seq -> open chromatin BED files)
These are derived from the same HuBMAP SNARE2 and 10X Multiome datasets that also contribute to CELLTYPE.
After cell type clustering, open chromatin peaks are called per cell type and exported as BED files.
These BED files slot into bed_annot.blade.php under a new "HuBMAP Open Chromatin" group.
Analogous to the existing "Brain Open Chromatin Atlas (BOCA)" group which has neuron/glia BED files per brain region.

column5: HuBMAP Open Chromatin - Kidney (N)
column7: Cell-type-specific open chromatin peaks called from SNARE2 ATAC modality. Number of BED files = number of distinct cell types identified after clustering. Will complement existing Roadmap E086 (Fetal Kidney, bulk).

column4: bed_annot.blade.php -> NEW group "HuBMAP Open Chromatin - Lung"
column5: HuBMAP Open Chromatin - Lung (N)
column7: Cell-type-specific peaks from SNARE2 ATAC. Complements existing Roadmap E096 (Lung, bulk) and E088 (Fetal Lung, bulk) in epi_options.

column5: HuBMAP Open Chromatin - Ovary (N)
column7: Cell-type-specific peaks from Multiome ATAC. Only existing Roadmap ovary entry is E097 (bulk). This would be the first cell-type-resolved ovary chromatin data in FUMA.

column1: SECTION 4: DATA PROCESSING PIPELINE REQUIRED (BEFORE WEBAPP CHANGES)

column5: Notes
All datasets are "protected". Required before any download.

column3: Per-dataset FASTQ or processed h5ad/loom files
column5: Prefer downloading already-processed h5ad (Scanpy AnnData) files if available to skip alignment

column4: Single merged AnnData object per tissue
column5: Concatenate all donors; retain donor ID as batch covariate
Filter low-quality cells: min genes, max % mitochondrial reads, doublet removal

column4: Clustered AnnData with cell type labels
column5: Normalize to 10k counts, log1p, PCA, UMAP, Leiden clustering; annotate cell types
Average log-normalised expression per gene per cell type; must be non-negative; shift by minimum

column3: Gene symbols or Ensembl v38 IDs from AnnData
column5: FUMA uses GRCh37 (hg19) Ensembl v92 by default; liftover required if data uses hg38 gene coords

column1: 8. (ATAC only) Call peaks per cell type
column5: For SNARE2 and Multiome datasets only; merge peaks across donors first
All FUMA genomic coordinates are hg19; HuBMAP data is aligned to hg38
Required format for FUMA BED annotation storage
